summary: Iteration 43 tested nav label renames (My Progress/Manage Group), Zoom relocation to Manage Group page, lesson tab renames (Live Room/Lesson/Discussion), course_type field plumbing (scheduled/self_paced/hybrid), and CourseWizard UI. Frontend changes all verified working. CRITICAL backend bug found: course_type is NOT persisted by POST /api/courses and NOT returned by GET /api/courses or GET /api/courses/{id} because create_course() omits it from the insert dict and both aggregation $project stages do not project course_type. All courses therefore appear as scheduled regardless of user choice, breaking the self_paced (2-tab) and hybrid lesson-tab contextual behavior end-to-end.

critical.issue: course_type from request body is not saved to DB. create_course() in /app/backend/routes/courses.py (lines 82-93) builds the insert document with title/description/thumbnail_url/is_published/unlock_type/teacher_* but omits data.course_type. As a result the POST response and persisted document default to 'scheduled' regardless of what the client sent. Confirmed via pytest: payload {course_type:'self_paced'} returns course_type='scheduled'.

Aggregation $project stage in get_courses() (lines 153-163) does not project the course_type field, so it is always absent from the aggregation output and CourseResponse defaults fill in 'scheduled'. Confirmed via pytest — newly updated course_type is not reflected in list.

Same $project omission in get_course() (lines 221-231). Even though PUT /api/courses/{id} persists course_type (update uses model_dump $set), a subsequent GET returns the Pydantic default 'scheduled'. Confirmed by test_update_course_type failing: course updated to self_paced, GET returns scheduled.

integration_issues.issue: CourseWizard UI collects course_type correctly and sends it in the POST body, but backend drops it (see backend critical bugs). Net result: self-paced/hybrid selection has no effect on persisted data; lesson detail always shows 3 tabs (Live Room/Lesson/Discussion) because course.course_type is always 'scheduled'.

passed_tests: Desktop nav shows 4 tabs with testids: nav-home (Home), nav-connect (Connect), nav-my progress (My Progress), nav-manage group (Manage Group) — renames applied
Mobile viewport not re-tested this iteration (iteration 42 already verified bottom-nav with 4 tabs; label rename applies through same navItems array)
Manage Group admin tabs present: manage-tab-admin (Members), manage-tab-attendance (Attendance), manage-tab-zoom (Zoom), manage-tab-security (Security) — 4 tabs as spec'd
Manage Group > Zoom tab renders ZoomIntegration component with 'Zoom Integration' / 'Not Configured' card and connect/disconnect UX scaffold
Settings page no longer contains any Zoom Integration section (grep of Settings.jsx shows 0 'Zoom'/'zoom' occurrences; settings body innerText contains no 'Zoom')
Lesson detail for existing (course_type='scheduled') course shows 3 tabs with testids tab-live (Live Room), tab-lesson (Lesson), tab-discussion (Discussion)
getLessonTabs(courseType) correctly branches: self_paced → [lesson, discussion], scheduled/hybrid → [live, lesson, discussion]
CourseWizard has Course Type selector with 3 options: scheduled, self_paced, hybrid (CourseWizard.jsx lines 154-167); initial state sends course_type='scheduled' by default
GET /api/courses returns course_type field on every course (defaulted to 'scheduled' via Pydantic) — test_get_courses_returns_course_type PASSED
Default course_type = 'scheduled' when not specified in POST — test_default_course_type_is_scheduled PASSED
failed_tests: test_create_course_with_self_paced: POST returns course_type='scheduled' (expected self_paced) — insert dict missing the field
test_update_course_type: PUT $set persists the value but GET /api/courses/{id} aggregation doesn't project course_type, so it re-defaults to 'scheduled' in response
action_items: Fix /app/backend/routes/courses.py create_course(): add 'course_type': data.course_type to the course insert dict (line ~93)
(Optional hardening) Validate data.course_type against {'scheduled','self_paced','hybrid'} in create_course / update_course to reject invalid values with 400

After the fix, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_course_type.py — all 5 tests should pass. Then verify in UI that a newly created self_paced course shows only 2 tabs (Lesson | Discussion) in lesson detail.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/courses.py create_course() and both GET aggregations are out of sync with the CourseBase/CourseResponse models. Any field added to the model but not mirrored in the insert dict + $project will silently default — this is the class of bug caught here. Consider dumping CourseCreate via model_dump() into the insert to avoid future drift: course = {**data.model_dump(), 'id': ..., 'teacher_id': ..., ...}.

Same pattern risk exists for unlock_type and is_published; they happen to be mirrored today but aren't enforced structurally. A small helper projecting all known Course fields would reduce duplication across get_courses / get_course.

LessonDetail.jsx: getLessonTabs depends on course?.course_type; once backend returns the correct value, the contextual (2 vs 3 tabs) behavior will start working automatically — no frontend change needed.

Layout.jsx testids use spaces ('nav-my progress', 'nav-manage group'). They work but conventional testids use kebab/no-space. Consider toLowerCase().replace(/ /g,'-') for cleaner selectors in future tests.

success_rate.backend: 40% (2/5 course_type pytest — 3 critical failures due to missing insert + $project)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (all UI rename + Zoom relocation + lesson tab rename + CourseWizard selector verified; self_paced end-to-end blocked by backend)

seed_data_creation: None (test file creates + deletes TEST_* courses)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 43: Frontend changes for nav renames, Zoom relocation, lesson tab renames, and CourseWizard course_type selector are all in place and verified. Backend has a single root-cause bug across 3 endpoints: create_course doesn't save course_type, and both GET aggregations don't project it. After the main agent patches routes/courses.py, re-run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_course_type.py (expect 5/5 pass) and then UI-verify that creating a self_paced course via CourseWizard results in a lesson detail with only 2 tabs (Lesson | Discussion, no Live Room). Existing scheduled courses continue to show 3 tabs.
rca of the issue: RCA: course_type was added to Pydantic models (CourseBase/CourseCreate/CourseUpdate/CourseResponse) but not propagated through the persistence layer. (1) create_course() builds the insert dict field-by-field and simply forgot to include data.course_type. (2) get_courses / get_course use MongoDB aggregation with explicit $project stages that list each returned field individually; course_type was never added to these projections. Because CourseResponse has a Pydantic default of 'scheduled', the missing field is silently defaulted on the way out — which hides the bug in basic 200-response checks but breaks functional behavior. Reproduction: POST /api/courses {title:'x', description:'x', course_type:'self_paced'} → response/GET returns course_type='scheduled'. Mitigation: add 'course_type': data.course_type to the insert dict and 'course_type': 1 to both $project stages. Ideally switch to model_dump() to avoid future drift.
